ddls:
CREATE TABLE "videos" (
  "video_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each video */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the video */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the video */,
  "thumbnail_url" TEXT /* Thumbnail image URL */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the channel that uploaded the video */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views the video has received */,
  "uploaded_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the video was uploaded */,
  "scraped_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the video data was scraped */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the video content */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the video in seconds */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the video */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the video */,
  "uploading_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the video */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("video_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_videos_uploading_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("uploading_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_videos_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES channels ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel */,
  "channel_description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the channel was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the channel was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., viewer, editor, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_category_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_category_mapping_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES video_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_category_mapping_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated video */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the video */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the video */,
  "dislikes" INTEGER /* Number of dislikes for the video */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares of the video */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the video */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Engagement score (calculated based on likes, dislikes, shares, and comments) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_analytics_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the video */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken (e.g., view, like, share) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag associated with videos */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_tag_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_tag_mapping_tag_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_id") REFERENCES video_tags ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_tag_mapping_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "social_media_platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media platform */,
  "platform_name" TEXT /* Name of the social media platform */,
  "platform_url" TEXT /* URL of the social media platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_shares" (
  "share_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each share record */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shared video */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* ID of the social media platform */,
  "share_time" TEXT /* Time of sharing */,
  "share_count" INTEGER /* Number of shares */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("share_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_shares_platform_id FOREIGN KEY ("platform_id") REFERENCES social_media_platforms ("platform_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_shares_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bulk_uploads" (
  "upload_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bulk upload */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the upload */,
  "upload_time" TEXT /* Time of upload */,
  "num_videos" INTEGER /* Number of videos uploaded in the batch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("upload_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bulk_uploads_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "video_id" INTEGER /* ID of the video being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user leaving the comment */,
  "comment_time" TEXT /* Time of comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_comments_video_id FOREIGN KEY ("video_id") REFERENCES videos ("video_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
